RustMinerSystem Core Server

Prepare the server program for mining access, third-party pool proxying, and PoolNode deployments. Choose the right system, architecture, and version, then install with a command or deploy a standalone file manually.

Read usage guideLatest Release v4.6.7
Miner access paths
Miner fleet
TCP / SSL direct
Optional RMS access
RustMinerSystemUnified access, hashrate allocation, and proxying
Unified accessHashrate allocationPool proxy
Route by configuration
Third-party pool Proxy
PoolNode real pool

Choose a server build

Get RustMinerSystem Detecting version for Linux x86

Open Linux standalone files on GitHub

Two installation methods are available below: beginners should start with the Linux command installer and follow the terminal prompts; standalone files are for users who want to configure startup, auto-start, and process supervision themselves.

Install with Linux command

Copy the command for x86 into your server terminal, then follow the prompts to complete installation.

Line 1
bash <(curl -s -L https://github.com/EvilGenius-dot/RustMinerSystem/raw/main/install.sh)
Line 2
bash <(curl -s -L -k https://rustminersystem.com/EvilGenius-dot/RustMinerSystem/raw/main/install.sh)

Standalone file download

For users familiar with deployment: download the standalone file, start it manually, then configure process supervision for your environment.

No downloadable files match the selected filters.

Ecosystem support

Infrastructure for downloads and deployment

RustMinerSystem works with cloud services, network infrastructure, and code hosting platforms to support reliable downloads, deployments, and global access.

VultrAmazon AWSMicrosoft AzureAlibaba Cloud InternationalCloudflareGitHub
RustMinerSystem

RustMinerSystem is built for operators who need more than a pool proxy: route miners to third-party pools with explicit hashrate skimming rules, launch a real PoolNode with settlement-layer fees, and add RMS encrypted compression when bandwidth or public connections become the bottleneck.
